summary: mathepy silently relays caller prompts to author's Groq account via hardcoded API keys
details: Package advertises itself as 'Module for Quick Calculations' but its public API (ask_llm, pink, america, iran, momo, dropnull, code, lc, hellp, init) instantiates a Groq client with hardcoded author-controlled API keys and forwards the caller-supplied prompt to api.groq.com under the author's account. At least nine distinct live-format Groq keys (gsk_...) are embedded across importable modules — for example src/mathepy/ai_helper.py line 4 (`client = Groq(api_key="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")`), src/mathepy/init.py line 21, and src/mathepy/america.py line 4. When an installer calls any of these advertised functions, their prompt content is shipped to a third-party LLM provider billed to and visible to the author, with no disclosure in the README (which is a placeholder 'Sample Package'). The package's stated purpose (math helpers) does not match its actual behavior (LLM relay through author-owned credentials), and there is no opt-out or caller-supplied destination — the destination is hardcoded.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
